evals.prompt: I'm building a PDP page and I want to add a custom 'Add to Wishlist' button in the product details drop-in, below the existing Add to Cart button. I don't want to fork the drop-in. How do I use slots to do this?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the slot pattern: passing a slots object to the ProductDetails component, using ctx.appendTo or ctx.replaceWith to inject custom DOM, and which slot to target (e.g., Actions or Footer). Should include a working code example.

evals.prompt: I need to add a product recommendation block on my homepage that shows 'Hot Sellers' in a 4-column grid layout. I've already created the recommendation unit in ACO and have the recId. Walk me through adding it to the DA page and setting up the grid CSS.
evals.expected_output: Should cover: DA table structure (Product Recommendations block with recId row), Section Metadata with a style class, and the CSS to override the default horizontal scroll into a 4-column grid. Should reference the product-recommendations block file.

evals.prompt: I want to override the GraphQL fragment used by the PDP drop-in to also fetch a custom attribute called 'warranty_info' so I can display it in the product description area. How do I do this without modifying the drop-in source files?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the fragment override pattern: importing setGQLQuery (or equivalent API) from the dropin, providing a custom fragment that includes the additional field, and then accessing it via ctx.data in a slot. Should warn that field names depend on the Magento schema.
